idea.hook: What if YOU could travel at the speed of light?
facts: The speed of light is a mind-boggling 670,616,629 miles per hour.
At light speed, you could zip from New York to Los Angeles (around 2,800 miles) in an astonishing 0.000015 seconds.
Even at this speed, reaching our Sun would still take over 8 minutes, and the nearest star, Proxima Centauri, would be a 4.2-year journey.
idea.payoff: But here's the kicker: even at light speed, crossing our own Milky Way galaxy would take a mind-bending 100,000 years. Makes you ponder cosmic distances, right?
idea.title: Light Speed! 🚀 How Fast is It REALLY?
idea.description: Imagine zooming across the universe at the ultimate speed! 🤯 What would that cosmic commute really look like? From zipping across the US in a blink to a multi-year trip to our nearest star, prepare for some mind-bending numbers. Will humanity ever reach light speed?

scenes.text: What if YOU traveled at light speed?
scenes.visual: A stylized spaceship or light streak zooming through a starfield, creating a warp effect.
A large speedometer graphic with the needle pointing to the maximum speed, superimposed over a fast-moving star background.

scenes.text: NYC to LA (2,800 miles) in 0.000015 seconds!
scenes.visual: An animated map of the USA, with a bright line zipping instantly from New York to Los Angeles.

scenes.text: To Sun: 8 mins. To Nearest Star: 4.2 Years!
scenes.visual: Visual starts with the Sun, then quickly zooms out to show Earth, then zooms further to reveal Proxima Centauri, with time labels appearing next to each destination.

scenes.text: Milky Way: 100,000 YEARS! Cosmic distances are mind-bending.
scenes.visual: A vast, slowly rotating animation of the entire Milky Way galaxy, emphasizing its immense size.
